task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenToonz window is visible with 'dwanko_run.tnz' displayed in the title bar, or the scene's drawings and timeline are visible in the workspace.
The Output Settings or Camera Settings window in OpenToonz shows the width set to 1080 and height set to 1920.

task_completion.description: Configure the frame rate to 30 FPS
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window in OpenToonz shows the Frame Rate (FPS) set to 30.

task_completion.description: Configure the output format to PNG
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window in OpenToonz shows the file format selected as 'png'.
The Output Settings window shows the 'Save In' or output path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/vertical_social/'.

task_completion.description: Render the sequence as PNG files
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows multiple PNG files (e.g., dwanko_run.0001.png) located in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/vertical_social/' directory, or a render progress bar in OpenToonz shows completion of at least 30 frames.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenToonz GUI to configure and render the scene
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with OpenToonz menus and dialogs (like Output Settings) rather than terminal commands like ffmpeg or ImageMagick.

integrity.description: Agent did not create mock or empty files
integrity.visual_evidence: The output directory contains actual image files (e.g., thumbnails visible in a file manager or file sizes > 0 in terminal) rather than empty files created with the 'touch' command.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual OpenToonz application
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application interface is clearly visible and actively used, not just a text editor modifying project files directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source scene is located at '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The expected output directory is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/vertical_social/'. The target resolution must be exactly 1080x1920 (width x height), and the frame rate must be 30 FPS. The output format is PNG. The final render should contain at least 30 frames and exceed 200 KB in total size.
